mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance against rate changes (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or 60-day forward returns turn negative when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during high-inflation regimes, or 20-day forward returns are negative when industrial production accelerates alongside rising CPI.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic resilience and supports consumer spending expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC remains below 0.01, or payroll growth consistently coincides with negative 5-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth combined with stable federal funds rates indicates accommodative financial conditions supporting economic expansion, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests, or 60-day returns are negative when GDP growth accelerates alongside steady policy rates.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and downstream consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing months.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating industrial production during periods of high unemployment indicates severe demand-supply mismatch and recessionary pressures, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive 5-day returns during high-unemployment periods, or the mechanism fails to predict direction when unemployment exceeds the 6% threshold.
